title: Hancea mengarawan
taxonomy: Hancea mengarawan is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Hancea, which is a group of plants that are native to the tropical regions of Asia. The specific classification of Hancea mengarawan is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Malpighiales, Family: Euphorbiaceae, Genus: Hancea, Species: H. mengarawan. This classification helps to identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and its place within the plant kingdom. Further research is needed to determine the full scope of Hancea mengarawan's classification and its relationships to other plants.

intro: Hancea mengarawan, also known as Mengarawan Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hancea mengarawan include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hancea mengarawan is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
